item.title: Live Updates: BTS Takes the Stage After Nearly 4 Years
item.description: K-pop’s biggest name will perform live in Seoul at 8 p.m. South Korea time (7 a.m. Eastern). The concert will be livestreamed by Netflix.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Launches Attacks in Lebanon as Iran Fires on U.S. Allies
item.description: The United States temporarily lifted sanctions on some Iranian oil in an effort to ease surging global oil prices.

item.title: 'BTS is everything for us': K-pop fans gather in Seoul for comeback show
item.description: Some 260,000 fans are expected to watch BTS perform together on Saturday for the first time since 2022.

item.title: Chart of the Week: Global rates show deeper war fears
item.description: Investors are becoming increasingly hawkish

item.title: Iran fires missiles at UK’s Diego Garcia base
item.description: Attack significantly exceeds Tehran’s previously known capabilities, stoking fears in Europe

item.title: Iranian Sailors, Including Survivors of Torpedoed Ship, Remain in Limbo
item.description: Sri Lanka has kept more than 250 sailors in protective custody since the early days of the war. Iran wants them back, but Sri Lanka is unsure what to do with them.

item.title: Zelensky Sends Negotiators to the U.S., Hoping to Revive Peace Talks
item.description: The Ukrainian president has said he has a “very bad feeling” about the effects of the Iran conflict on Ukraine’s own war.

item.title: Fidel Castro’s Grandson Flaunts Beer, Nikes and Trump Jokes on Instagram
item.description: Sandro Castro has drawn scrutiny for his Instagram posts in which he flaunts a life of luxury while using satire to point out the very deterioration his family’s leadership helped create.

item.title: No Pills or Needles, Just Paper: How Deadly Drugs Are Changing
item.description: Lab-made drugs soaked into the pages of letters, books and even legal documents are being smuggled behind bars, killing inmates and frustrating investigators.

item.title: Russian drone attack kills two in Ukraine ahead of talks in US, officials say
item.description: Two children, aged 11 and 15 were also injured in the attack which took place in Zaporizhzhia.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
The Road to the Battle of Hastings in 1066? Maybe Not a Road After All.
item.description: New research suggests that a legendary 200-mile march by Harold Godwinson, the last Anglo-Saxon king of England, may have actually involved some ships.

item.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
item.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

item.title: BTS Arirang review: K-pop idols rekindle their fire
item.description: The band's 10th album sees them return to the rebellious, rap-heavy sound of their early days.

item.title: BTS comeback concert: Seoul gets ready to host 260,000 fans
item.description: RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook will perform together for the first time since October 2022.

item.title: Watch: Thick smoke billows from South Korea car parts plant in deadly fire
item.description: Eleven people were killed and dozens injured when a huge blaze engulfed a car parts factory in the central city of Daejeon.

item.title: Iran lets grain ships through Hormuz to shore up food supply
item.description: Agriculture has been a ‘pain point’ for Tehran after it imposes de facto blockade of waterway in response to US-Israeli war

item.title: Energy crisis upends Rachel Reeves’ growth plan amid hints of recession
item.description: Chancellor had vowed to cut cost of living but Treasury now foresees ‘very difficult Budget’ later this year

item.title: Iran launches 10mn rial banknote as war triggers dash for cash
item.description: Iranians queue to get hold of highest-ever denomination bills as US-Israeli strikes fuel demand for hard currency

item.title: Iran war raises the risk of a bond market shock
item.description: Wild swings in UK gilts point to strains that will hurt government finances and jack up borrowing costs for us all

item.title: Uber fights to stay in the robotaxi race
item.description: Ride-hailing company must make up lost ground on driverless vehicles, or risk being left in the rear-view mirror

item.title: Life insurance sales surge in wake of Reeves’ inheritance tax shake-up
item.description: Chancellor’s targeting of pensions prompts more people to take out insurance, say industry figures

item.title: EU member states urged to lower gas storage targets due to Iran war
item.description: European Commission proposes steps to try to reduce demand for energy from households and industry

item.title: History is tragically repeating itself in Lebanon
item.description: Ensuring the country’s survival — and Hizbollah’s weakening — is Trump’s best hope for Middle East success

item.title: Is Britain ready for US-style religious politics?
item.description: Fuelled by new funding and transatlantic links, Christian groups are playing an increasingly prominent role on the UK right

item.title: Ten Thousand Things, the cult New York jeweller you need to know
item.description: Inside the atelier making art with uncut gems

item.title: HMRC ‘unlikely to be lenient’ with tax exiles fleeing Dubai, advisers warn
item.description: Returning Britons could face hit from both income tax and capital gains tax

item.title: Britain must be more vigilant to the risk of sabotage by hostile states
item.description: If Moscow and Beijing are determined to destabilise the west then new strategies are needed

item.title: The new old world
item.description: A Gulf war, an oil spike, a tense Europe: the 20th century is back

item.title: Several projects in Starmer’s flagship ‘new towns’ programme deemed unviable
item.description: Ministers conclude that some of these projects cannot go ahead and will be ‘deprioritised’

item.title: The Israeli border towns driving Netanyahu’s hard line on Lebanon
item.description: Government is pushing a hyper-aggressive military strategy against Hizbollah to avoid mass evacuations in the country’s north

item.title: Is it ever right to give one child more money than another?
item.description: When it comes to gifts or inheritance, parents must decide whether to give equally, or help those in most need

item.title: Architect Shin Chang’s guide to Kuala Lumpur
item.description: For the KL-born creative, the Malaysian capital is modern while staying true to its roots

item.title: The Vineta: Palm Beach’s new beacon for billionaires
item.description: After years of delays, the century-old hotel relaunches this month as a first US foray for the company behind France’s celebrated Hôtel du Cap

item.title: Chelsea Chief Under Abramovich Could Face Scrutiny Over Soccer Cheating
item.description: A former key aide to the Russian oligarch would face an investigation into her time overseeing the Premier League club should she return to soccer.

item.title: A Public Holiday Every Week? Sri Lankans Bemoan the Enforced Day Off.
item.description: The island’s government, rationing fuel amid shortages from the Iran war, surprised residents by imposing an unusual four-day week.

item.title: ​At Least 10 Killed in South Korean Factory Fire
item.description: Survivors jumped from windows to escape the blaze, the deadliest at a factory in South Korea in two years. Four people are still missing.

item.title: Trump Signals Retreat From Goal of Regime Change in Iran
item.description: In the first days of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, Mr. Trump pressed for a popular uprising against Tehran’s government.

item.title: Russia's school propaganda was highlighted by Oscar-winning film - but does it work?
item.description: The messages are clear: the full-scale invasion is a defensive war and patriotism means unquestionable loyalty.

item.title: Socialists battle to hold Paris in key mayoral elections across France
item.description: Emmanuel Grégoire is facing a challenge from Rachida Dati on the right who is behind in the opinion polls.

item.title: The forgotten Indian woman trailblazer in British medicine
item.description: In 1912, Jamini Sen became the first woman Fellow of Glasgow’s Royal College, founded in 1599 and long closed to women.

item.title: The fight to control the narrative in the Afghan-Pakistan conflict
item.description: The battle between the neighbouring countries is taking place as much online as it is on the ground.

item.title: Seeking to Rely Less on China, U.S. Pushes a Rare Earths Partnership on a Reluctant Brazil
item.description: Seeking to reduce its reliance on China, the United States is pushing for a critical minerals deal with Brazil. The South American country is less eager.

item.title: A Look at the Escalating Battle for the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: There are several ways the United States could attempt to reopen the waterway, all of which carry substantial risks.

item.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
item.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

item.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
item.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Targets to Try to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Israeli military said it had killed the spokesman for Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, and Iran warned that it could target American and Israeli military personnel.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran in Its Third Week
item.description: The Persian new year and the end of the holy month of Ramadan were marked by continuing strikes in the region as the war reached the end of its third week.

item.title: Ukraine-Hungary oil pipeline row threatens EU loan
item.description: Kyiv and Budapest disagree on how badly damaged a hub in western Ukraine bombed by Russia really is, as its oil flow remains suspended.

item.title: On a Holiday of Renewal, Iranians Are Mourning and Fearful
item.description: As they start a new year, Iranians are reckoning with bombardment, repression and economic misery. Still, many are holding fast to ancient traditions.

item.title: Starmer most popular PM - in Toby Jug sales
item.description: Toby Jugs depicting Sir Keir outsold all other prime ministers in the Parliament shop in 2025.

item.title: Reform UK suspends Scotland candidate over financial allegations
item.description: Scotland leader Malcolm Offord told BBC Radio Scotland that the party had "spent a lot of time on vetting" candidates

item.title: Farage 'will fight' Essex council reorganisation
item.description: A senior Conservative councillor says the Reform UK leader "knows nothing" about local government.

item.title: A Diary of War From an Unlikely Author: the Son of Iran’s President
item.description: In an online journal, Yousef Pezeshkian offers readers a mix of personal anecdotes and glimpses behind the scenes as Iranian leaders are picked off one after another.

item.title: Hillsborough Law to be further delayed
item.description: The prime minister had promised to pass the legislation by 15 April 2025, to mark the 36th anniversary of the disaster.

item.title: Border security chief to step down after 18 months in the job
item.description: Martin Hewitt had been tasked with cutting the numbers trying to get to the UK in small boats.

item.title: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
item.description: Syria condemned the strikes on weapons sites and government infrastructure as an "outrageous" assault on Syria's sovereignty.

item.title: Neighbourhoods to get £20m funding boost
item.description: Local people can help decide where the £20m over a decade should go, the government says.

item.title: Norway's crown princess breaks silence, claiming she was 'manipulated and deceived' by Epstein
item.description: Crown Princess Mette Marit tells Norwegian TV she wishes she had never met the late sex offender.

item.title: UK borrowing costs hit highest level since 2008 financial crisis
item.description: The interest rate on government debt is climbing over fears about inflation, interest rates, and public spending, experts have said.

item.title: Iran warns UK letting US use bases is 'participation in aggression'
item.description: Iran's foreign minister urges his UK counterpart Yvette Cooper to end cooperation with the US.

item.title: 'Multiple factors' caused 2025 Spain and Portugal blackout, says report
item.description: A new report has determined the causes of the unprecedented blackout which left the two countries without power for several hours last year.

item.title: Car stolen with two-month-old baby inside
item.description: The baby boy was rescued by police unharmed and reunited with his mother shortly after the ordeal.

item.title: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
item.description: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

item.title: Reform-led council approves councillor wage rise
item.description: It is "bad optics" to "award ourselves a nice little pay rise", a Green Party councillor says.

item.title: Venezuelan leader replaces senior military commanders
item.description: The reshuffle follows Delcy Rodríguez's appointment of a new defence minister, a move human rights groups described as a "recycling of impunity".

item.title: Mexican teen dies while being held in US immigration custody
item.description: Royer Perez-Jimenez died amid a surge of migrant deaths under Donald Trump's immigration crackdown.

item.title: Farage pauses Cameo account for security reasons, says Reform UK
item.description: It comes after a Guardian investigation suggests the party leader recorded supportive messages for a convicted rioter and a neo-Nazi event.

item.title: Trump makes Pearl Harbor remark in meeting with Japan's PM
item.description: President Trump compared US strikes on Iran to the deadly 1941 Japanese attack and declaration of war as part of World War Two.

item.title: Labour MPs threaten vote to show opposition to Mahmood's migration plans
item.description: Opponents are considering using parliamentary procedure to force a vote to highlight their concerns.

item.title: Trump Organization Eyes Transylvania Golf Course Deal
item.description: The president’s company is eyeing a site for a golf course and luxury apartments. Will a huge landfill and a troubled project history stand in the way?

item.title: I want to hold balance of power at next general election, says Zack Polanski
item.description: Green Party leader says thought of becoming PM is 'on his mind' and the aim of 30-40 MPs now feels "under ambitious".

item.title: US deports Singaporean vlogger convicted of child sex offences
item.description: Many Singaporeans remember Amos Yee for his rants insulting state authorities and religion.

item.title: Taxpayers to fund clear-up of huge illegal waste dumps
item.description: The government says it will step in to clean up some of the biggest illegal rubbish tips in England.

item.title: Australia PM heckled at Sydney mosque Ramadan event
item.description: The government's response to the Israel-Gaza war and rising Islamophobia has angered many Muslim Australians.

item.title: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
item.description: Some 26 million people are "facing extreme hunger" in Kenya, Ethiopia and Somalia, warns humanitarian organisation Oxfam.

item.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
item.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

item.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
item.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
item.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

item.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
item.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

item.title: Denmark planned to blow up Greenland runways if US invaded, reports say
item.description: Denmark's public broadcaster reports that the US operation to seize Venezuela's leader sparked fears it would act against Greenland.

item.title: Racism behind rise in support for Reform, says Swinney
item.description: The first minister told the BBC's Scotcast the views of some Reform UK supporters were "intolerant".

item.title: Reform UK's Scottish manifesto pledges analysed
item.description: BBC Scotland journalists have analysed some of the policies in Reform's manifesto for the 2026 Holyrood election.

item.title: UK reveals aid priorities after major cuts to budget
item.description: Aid groups and MPs have raised concerns over the move to prioritise defence spending over aid.

item.title: UK agrees deal to ease migrant returns to Nigeria
item.description: It comes during the Nigerian president's state visit to the UK - the first by a west African leader in 37 years.

item.title: Badenoch backs Tory MP in row over Muslim prayer event
item.description: The Tory leader has faced calls to sack a colleague who called public prayers an "act of domination".

item.title: Killer refused family plea before death - inquest
item.description: Bradley Murdoch, who murdered British backpacker Peter Falconio, never revealed where his body was.

item.title: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
item.description: Caf executive committee member Augustin Senghor says the decision to strip Senegal of the 2025 Afcon title is "unacceptable, abject and we have to denounce it".

item.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
item.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

item.title: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
item.description: President Idris Mahamat Déby orders the military to be on high alert and a "total closure" of the border.

item.title: Tories only party with a plan, says Badenoch as she launches election campaign
item.description: Speaking ahead of the 7 May poll, the Tory leader says her party is the only one "that can actually fix things".

item.title: Spain's king welcomes Mexico's World Cup invite after 'abuse' comments
item.description: News of the invitation emerges after the monarch acknowledged "a lot of abuse" during the Spanish conquest of Mexico.

item.title: Viktor Orban’s pro-natalist policies are not working
item.description: Despite lavish spending on mothers, Hungary’s fertility rate is falling again

item.title: The Iran war is forcing Europe to confront its energy problem
item.description: High power prices are putting the continent’s economy at risk

item.title: Israel contemplates a ground invasion of Lebanon
item.description: That could wreck Lebanon’s chances of taking on Hizbullah itself

item.title: A shake-up at Africa’s spikiest media group
item.description: Can the new owner preserve its independent spirit?

item.title: The war in eastern Congo is escalating far from view
item.description: Fighting in South Kivu is a disaster for civilians and may threaten the copper belt

item.title: Life in Myanmar’s biggest city is increasingly grim
item.description: A new rubber-stamp parliament will solve nothing

item.title: Cuba’s broken economy leaves it at Donald Trump’s mercy
item.description: He seems more interested in creating a client state than a free Cuba

item.title: Why China’s fight on air pollution has slowed
item.description: Officials are now trying to take it to the next stage

item.title: Public opinion in China is hardening on America and Taiwan
item.description: A rare poll shows the views of ordinary Chinese are changing

item.title: Africa after aid is more resilient than you might think
item.description: But more needs to be done to ensure a prosperous future

item.title: Gas will not be killed off by renewables any time soon
item.description: But there are ways to rely less on it

item.title: Lebanon’s leaders must take on Hizbullah
item.description: And Israel must not play the spoiler

item.title: Cyprus leader calls for frank discussion on 'colonial' UK bases
item.description: The UK's two military bases on Cyprus are a "colonial consequence" on the island, says President Nikos Christodoulides.

item.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
item.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

item.title: A deadly strike in Kabul could have big knock-on effects
item.description: The war between Afghanistan and Pakistan is growing ever more lethal

item.title: Panicked Indians are scrambling to buy gas
item.description: War over Iran threatens the fuel that powers hundreds of millions of stoves

item.title: DR Congo and Rwanda agree to ease tensions after talks in US
item.description: The conflict has persisted despite a peace deal signed by DR Congo and Rwanda in December.

item.title: War in Iran is making Donald Trump weaker—and angrier
item.description: By diminishing the president’s political superpowers, his reckless campaign may make him more dangerous

item.title: Police investigating bailout email from Mandelson to Epstein
item.description: Met chief Sir Mark Rowley says police are looking at whether the email was a criminal offence.

item.title: Ecuador gang leader wanted for murder of presidential candidate arrested
item.description: Officials believe the man - known as Lobo Menor - is linked to the killing of Fernando Villavicencio in 2023.

item.title: India's ceramic hub grinds to a halt as Iran war chokes gas supply
item.description: Manufacturers stopped operations after supplies of propane and natural gas reduced sharply in recent days.

item.title: Olympic cyclist who ran over wife under fire over Porsche post
item.description: The post was criticised for being "deeply offensive" by a victims' rights commissioner.

item.title: Indonesia detains four soldiers over acid attack on activist
item.description: Andrie Yunus, who criticised the army's role in government, suffered burns to 20% of his body.

item.title: South Africans say criminal gangs are exploiting the water crisis
item.description: Residents of some areas have been without mains water for weeks.

item.title: How Europe sleepwalked into yet another energy crisis
item.description: It is not the first time that there has been deep energy-linked frustration in the heart of Europe.

item.title: Watch: Real possum spotted on toy animal shelf at Australian airport
item.description: Passengers discovered a real possum nestled among other native Australian toy animals at Hobart Airport, Tasmania.

item.title: As Cuba struggles with power cuts, how is the island holding up?
item.description: The BBC's Will Grant reports from the island days after the entire national electric grid collapsed.

item.title: Afghan evacuees in limbo in Qatar camp accuse US of betrayal
item.description: More than 1,100 Afghans evacuated by the US fear their route to promised resettlement is closed for good.

item.title: How Finnish supermarkets are central to the country's defence
item.description: The chains all have detailed plans to follow in the event of the nation going to war.

item.title: India's young are more educated than ever. So why are so many jobless?
item.description: India’s educational enrolment has surged but jobs, especially good ones, are not keeping pace, a new report finds.

item.title: 'One of a kind' Messi hits 900 career goals - numbers behind milestone
item.description: After Lionel Messi scored his 900th career goal on Wednesday, BBC Sport looks at the numbers behind the "insane" achievement.

item.title: The Iran war is causing a global energy crisis - can China withstand it?
item.description: With oil supply disrupted, Beijing's oil reserves and renewable energy push are being put to the test.

item.title: How Ukraine and Europe got caught in a geopolitical lovers’ tiff
item.description: A testing moment for steadfast allies

item.title: Senegal government alleges corruption over Afcon
item.description: The Senegalese government calls for an "independent international investigation" at the Confederation of African Football after it stripped Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations title.

item.title: Is cheap energy the key to China gaining AI supremacy?
item.description: Leaders are hoping it will make up for its weakness in chips

item.title: One killed as ski gondola crashes down mountain at Swiss resort
item.description: Dramatic footage showed the gondola flipping over several times in the snow before coming to a halt in Engelberg.

item.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
item.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

item.title: Russian archaeologist can be sent to Ukraine for trial, Polish judge rules
item.description: Archaeologist Alexander Butyagin is accused of conducting illegal excavations in Crimea - Ukraine's peninsula annexed by Russia.

item.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
item.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

item.title: Celebrations in Morocco but devastation in Senegal after Afcon decision
item.description: Moroccans feel justice has been done but the Senegalese are angry after being stripped of the title.

item.title: 'The longest VAR check in history' - the fallout from shock Afcon decision
item.description: BBC Sport journalists and pundits look at what happened at the chaotic Afcon final, and what comes next.

item.title: A dirty deal with Cuba would be better than the alternatives
item.description: A prolonged blockade risks creating a humanitarian crisis on America’s doorstep

item.title: Venezuelans celebrate historic World Baseball Classic victory over USA
item.description: Venezuela won its first-ever World Baseball Classic title, defeating the United States 3–2 in the championship game.

item.title: Hittler faces Zielinski as French town's election contest goes viral
item.description: Charles Hittler, mayor of Arcis-sur-Aube, has always faced jokes about his name but now he faces a rival with another unlikely name.

item.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
item.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

item.title: Argentina make pitch to host 2035 Rugby World Cup
item.description: Argentina is pressing forward with a bid to host the 2035 Rugby World Cup, which would take the event to South America for the first time.

item.title: Afcon final chaos - key questions answered
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at why the 2025 Afcon final result has been overturned - and what it means.

item.title: Middle East Dispatch newsletter: Iran’s mood shifts
item.description: The immediate imperative is no longer political change but personal safety, writes Nick Pelham, our Middle East correspondent

item.title: A spy scandal upends Slovenia’s election campaign
item.description: Embarrassing videos and a mysterious Israeli intelligence firm

item.title: Why ex-Palace winger Olise is now in Ballon d'Or contention at Bayern
item.description: Michael Olise has been in superb form for Bayern Munich this season, has the French winger put himself in contention for the Ballon d'Or?
